questions.question: The graph of y = 3sin(2x) has a period of what value?
questions.explanation: For y = A·sin(Bx), the period is 2π/B. Here B = 2, so period = 2π/2 = π.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 40°, angle B = 75°, and side a = 10. Using the Law of Sines, find side b to the nearest whole number.
questions.explanation: Law of Sines: a/sin(A) = b/sin(B). Thus b = a·sin(B)/sin(A) = 10·sin(75°)/sin(40°) ≈ 10·(0.966)/(0.643) ≈ 15.

questions.question: What is the amplitude of the function y = –2cos(x) + 5?
questions.explanation: Amplitude is the absolute value of the coefficient of the trig function. Here |–2| = 2. The +5 is a vertical shift, not part of amplitude.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, p = 8, q = 6, and angle R = 50°. Using the Law of Cosines, find side r to the nearest tenth.
The function y = tan(x) has a vertical asymptote at x = π/2. Name one other vertical asymptote of this function.
questions.explanation: tan(x) has vertical asymptotes at odd multiples of π/2. Both 3π/2 and –π/2 are correct; accept either or other odd multiples.

questions.question: A triangle has sides of length 5, 7, and 8. Using the Law of Cosines, find the measure of the angle opposite the side of length 7, to the nearest degree.
questions.explanation: Let the angle opposite side 7 be B. By Law of Cosines: 7² = 5² + 8² – 2(5)(8)cos(B), so 49 = 89 – 80cos(B), giving cos(B) = 0.5, thus B ≈ 58°.
